title: Scrapy Architecture, Scheduler, Middleware, AutoThrottle, and Observability
description: A hand-built crawler grows hidden hooks, middleware order changes behavior, duplicate filters lose identity, AutoThrottle is treated as permission, and metrics cannot reconstruct failures. Learners cumulatively produce Scrapy 2.17 scale and observability transfer plan through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace engine, scheduler, downloader, spider, item pipeline, signals, extensions, requests, responses, failures, and ownership in Scrapy.
Map canonical request identity, priorities, persistence, depth, retries, duplicate filtering, and resume semantics into scheduler evidence.
Reason about request and response traversal order, built-in middleware, exceptions, short-circuiting, and settings merge before customization.
